title: Illuminated Logo Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale face-lit illuminated logo boxes with LED illumination and UL 48 listing. German-engineered contour cabinets with 3-week lead times for sign trade only.
heroSubtitle: Face-lit contour logo cabinets that turn your client's brand into a luminous landmark. UL 48 listed, German-engineered, and delivered in three weeks.

sections.heading: Why Illuminated Logo Boxes Dominate Brand Visibility
sections.content:
Illuminated logo boxes — sometimes called cloud logos or contour cabinets — are the premium choice when a brand identity needs to command attention — whether paired with front-lit channel letters or standing alone day and night. Unlike channel letters that spell out a name character by character, a logo box reproduces the entire brand mark as a single, cohesive unit. The face is typically translucent acrylic or polycarbonate with digitally printed or vinyl-applied graphics, backlit by high-output LED modules that push consistent, even light across the entire logo surface.

This format is particularly effective for brands with complex marks — think multi-color gradients, intricate illustrations, or wordmarks with fine serifs that would be impossible to execute as individual channel letters. By housing the entire logo inside a single contour-cut aluminum cabinet, you get faithful color reproduction during daylight hours and a dramatic illuminated presence after dark. The result is a sign that works twice as hard as a non-lit alternative.

At Sunlite Signs, we manufacture every illuminated logo box from scratch in our own facility. Our German-engineered fabrication standards mean precision-welded aluminum frames, optically calibrated LED spacing for zero hot spots, and UL 48 certification on every unit. We sell exclusively to sign shops and trade professionals — your clients stay yours, your margins stay healthy, and the product quality speaks for itself.

sections.heading: Construction Specifications & LED Engineering
sections.content:
Every Sunlite illuminated logo box begins with a CNC-routed aluminum frame that is welded, ground smooth, and finished to automotive-grade standards. Our complimentary engineering covers every structural detail. Cabinet depths typically range from 3.5" to 8" depending on the logo complexity and required illumination uniformity. Deeper cabinets allow greater LED-to-face distance, which improves light mixing and eliminates the hot-spotting that plagues cheaply built logo signs.

  • Face Options: 3mm or 4.5mm white acrylic with first- or second-surface digital print, or translucent vinyl overlay on opal polycarbonate for higher impact resistance
  • LED Modules: Samsung or Nichia LEDs at 160+ lumens per watt, spaced on a calculated grid for uniform luminance. Available in 6500K, 5000K, 4000K, or RGB for color-changing effects
  • Power Supplies: UL-recognized Mean Well constant-voltage drivers, mounted on a removable service tray for easy field maintenance
  • Finish: Two-stage polyurethane paint system in any PMS, RAL, or custom color match. Textured and metallic finishes available

We engineer each cabinet with rear-access service panels so your install crew can reach the electrical components without removing the sign face. Every unit ships fully assembled, burned-in for 24 hours, and tested for consistent illumination before it leaves our facility. Standard production is three weeks from approved artwork.

sections.heading: Applications & Brand Use Cases
sections.content:
Illuminated logo boxes are found across virtually every commercial vertical because they solve a universal problem: reproducing a complex brand mark at scale with both daytime accuracy and nighttime impact. Here are the most common applications — all supported by our wholesale manufacturing our trade partners encounter:

  • QSR and restaurant chains: Multi-color mascot logos and crests that can't be reduced to simple channel letters
  • Healthcare and medical campuses: Shield-style logos with gradients, icons, and taglines in a single cabinet
  • Automotive dealerships: Manufacturer-spec logo reproduction with exact color matching and brightness requirements
  • Hospitality: Boutique hotel and resort branding where the logo itself is the architectural statement
  • Retail and mixed-use developments: Anchor tenant branding on pylons and monument bases

Because each logo box is custom-fabricated to match the specific brand mark, Sunlite Signs works closely with your design team during pre-production. We provide engineering drawings, color proofs, and structural calculations so you can present a professional package to your end client. Our in-house engineering team can also advise on optimal cabinet depth, LED color temperature, and face material selection to achieve the best possible result for a given application.

faqs.question: What is a logo box sign?
faqs.answer: A logo box — also called a cloud logo or contour cabinet — is a custom-shaped aluminum sign cabinet that reproduces a brand's logo as a single illuminated unit. The cabinet silhouette is CNC-cut to match the logo's outline, with a translucent face that is backlit by internal LED modules for day-and-night visibility.

faqs.question: Are your illuminated logo boxes UL listed?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every illuminated logo box we manufacture carries UL 48 certification, covering the complete assembly including the LED system and power supply. This is required for electrical code compliance in most U.S. jurisdictions.

faqs.question: How do you ensure even illumination across the logo face?
faqs.answer: We calculate LED module spacing based on cabinet depth and face material translucency. Our engineering team uses optical modeling to map the light distribution, ensuring zero hot spots or dark zones across the entire logo surface.

faqs.question: Can you match exact PMS or brand-standard colors?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use high-resolution digital printing or precision-cut translucent vinyl to reproduce exact PMS, RAL, or brand-standard colors on the sign face. Daylight and illuminated color proofs are available upon request.

faqs.question: Do you sell illuminated logo boxes to end users?
faqs.answer: No. Sunlite Signs is a wholesale-only manufacturer. We sell exclusively to licensed sign companies and trade professionals. Your client relationship stays yours — we never compete with our trade partners.

title: Halo-Lit Logo Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale halo-lit logo boxes with reverse LED illumination for an upscale silhouette glow. German-engineered, UL 48 listed, 3-week delivery for sign trade.
heroSubtitle: Reverse-illuminated contour logo cabinets that create a dramatic halo glow against any surface. The premium choice for luxury and corporate branding.

sections.heading: The Visual Impact of Halo-Lit Logo Signs
sections.content:
Halo-lit logo boxes produce one of the most sophisticated lighting effects in the sign industry. Instead of illuminating the face of the sign, LED modules are mounted behind the cabinet and aimed toward the mounting surface — typically a wall, fascia, or panel. The result is a soft, diffused halo of light that silhouettes the logo shape, creating an elegant floating effect that is immediately associated with premium brands.

This reverse-illumination approach works exceptionally well for brands that want nighttime presence without the aggressive brightness of face-lit signage. The logo itself appears as a solid, opaque shape during the day — showcasing precise paint finishes and dimensional form — while the halo effect transforms it into a luminous statement after dark. It's a dual-personality sign that performs beautifully in both conditions.

At Sunlite Signs, our halo-lit logo boxes are engineered with calculated standoff depths that optimize the light wash across the mounting surface. Too close and you get harsh lines; too far and the glow dissipates. We model the LED placement and standoff distance for each project individually, ensuring the halo reads cleanly at the intended viewing distance. Every unit is UL 48 listed and manufactured exclusively for sign trade professionals.

sections.heading: Reverse Illumination Engineering & Specs
sections.content:
Halo-lit logo boxes require a different engineering approach than face-lit units. The light source must project outward from the rear of the cabinet and create a uniform wash against the mounting surface, which means LED placement, beam angle, and standoff distance — all covered by our complimentary engineering all need to be precisely calibrated.

  • Cabinet Construction: Welded aluminum with a fully opaque face — no translucent panels. The rear is open or fitted with a perforated backing to allow light projection. Standard depths range from 3" to 6"
  • LED Configuration: High-output modules with wide beam angles (120° to 160°) mounted on the interior perimeter of the cabinet, aimed rearward. Typical LED color temperature is 6500K for a crisp white halo, though warm white (3000K–4000K) is popular for hospitality projects
  • Standoff System: Adjustable aluminum standoffs, typically 1.5" to 3" from the mounting surface, engineered for the specific cabinet size and wall material. Concealed wiring routes through the standoffs to the power supply
  • Power Supply: UL-recognized Mean Well drivers, either mounted behind the sign or remotely located in an accessible junction box

We finish every halo-lit cabinet with our standard automotive-grade polyurethane paint system, available in any PMS, RAL, or custom color. Because the face is opaque, the daytime appearance is all about the paint quality and the precision of the contour cut — both areas where our German-engineered fabrication process excels.

sections.heading: Where Halo-Lit Logo Boxes Excel
sections.content:
Halo-lit logo boxes are the default choice for brands and environments where subtlety and sophistication outweigh raw brightness. They communicate quality without shouting — similar to stainless steel flat cut letters, which is why they're heavily specified in these verticals:

  • Luxury retail and fashion: High-end boutiques, jewelry stores, and designer showrooms where the brand aesthetic demands understated elegance
  • Corporate headquarters and lobbies: Tech firms, financial institutions, and professional services offices where a polished, modern identity is paramount
  • Hospitality: Upscale hotels, resorts, and restaurants that use lighting as a design element rather than just a functional tool
  • Medical and wellness facilities: Hospitals, clinics, and spas that benefit from a calming, non-glaring sign presence

One important consideration for your client proposals: halo-lit logo boxes perform best against smooth, light-colored surfaces that reflect the LED wash effectively. Dark or heavily textured walls absorb light and reduce the halo effect. If the installation surface is dark, we can recommend supplemental techniques like a painted backer panel or increased LED output to compensate. Our engineering team is always available to consult on surface-specific recommendations during the quoting phase.

faqs.question: What is the difference between halo-lit and face-lit logo boxes?
faqs.answer: Face-lit logo boxes illuminate the front of the sign through a translucent face panel, making the logo itself glow. Halo-lit logo boxes project light behind the cabinet toward the mounting surface, creating a silhouette glow effect around the logo's perimeter. Halo-lit is considered more upscale and subtle.

faqs.question: What wall surface works best for halo-lit signs?
faqs.answer: Smooth, light-colored surfaces such as painted stucco, white ACM panels, or light stone produce the best halo effect because they reflect LED light efficiently. Dark or rough surfaces absorb light, reducing the glow. We can advise on LED output adjustments for non-ideal surfaces.

faqs.question: Can a logo box be both halo-lit and face-lit?
faqs.answer: Yes. We manufacture combination units that illuminate both the face and the halo simultaneously. This dual-lit approach maximizes day-and-night brand visibility and is popular for high-traffic commercial locations that need maximum impact.

faqs.question: What standoff depth do you recommend for halo-lit logo boxes?
faqs.answer: Standoff depth depends on cabinet size and desired halo width. Generally, 1.5" to 3" provides the best balance. Our engineering team models the optimal standoff distance for each project based on the logo dimensions and mounting surface.

faqs.question: Are halo-lit logo boxes suitable for outdoor installations?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Our halo-lit cabinets are built with welded aluminum construction, sealed wiring penetrations, and weather-rated LED modules. They're engineered to withstand rain, UV exposure, and temperature extremes in any exterior environment.

title: Franchise Logo Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale franchise logo signs and multi-location logo boxes. Consistent brand execution across every site. UL 48 listed, 3-week delivery for sign trade only.
heroSubtitle: Consistent brand execution across every franchise location. Production-ready logo boxes built for national rollouts and multi-site programs.

sections.heading: Why Franchise Brands Demand Logo Box Consistency
sections.content:
Franchise systems live and die by brand consistency. When a customer visits a location in Dallas, the sign on the building should look identical to the one they recognize from Chicago. Logo boxes are the preferred sign format for franchise brands precisely because they reproduce the full brand mark — colors, proportions, icon, and wordmark — as a single, controlled unit. Unlike channel letters that can vary in font rendering or spacing across vendors, a logo box ensures pixel-level fidelity to the brand standard every time.

The challenge for sign shops servicing franchise accounts is maintaining that consistency across dozens or hundreds of locations while managing cost and lead times. That's where Sunlite Signs becomes your competitive advantage. We set up each franchise logo as a production-ready template in our system — pre-engineered for cabinet dimensions, LED layout, color specifications, and mounting details. Once the template is locked in, every reorder is a repeat build with zero re-engineering time and guaranteed identical output.

Our wholesale-only model means you maintain your direct relationship with the franchise operator or property management group. We never solicit your accounts, never put our name on the product, and never compete with your bids. You get German-engineered quality, UL 48 compliance, and three-week production on every unit — the kind of reliability that wins national account contracts.

sections.heading: Program Management & Production Efficiency
sections.content:
Running a multi-location sign program requires more than just building good signs — it demands engineering, logistics, and documentation — and repeatability. Sunlite Signs supports franchise programs with a structured workflow designed to minimize your administrative overhead:

  • Master Template Files: We store approved engineering drawings, LED maps, paint specifications, and face artwork for each franchise brand. Reorders reference the template number, eliminating redundant design work
  • Batch Production: For rollouts of 10+ locations, we schedule batch fabrication runs that reduce per-unit cost and compress delivery timelines. Batch orders of 20+ units can ship in as few as two weeks
  • Consistent Quality Control: Every unit is built against the same shop drawings and inspected against the same QC checklist, regardless of whether it's order number one or number fifty
  • Documentation Packages: Each shipment includes installation drawings, wiring diagrams, and mounting templates specific to the franchise specification

We also handle site-specific variations within a franchise program — different mounting heights, single-face versus double-face configurations, or size adjustments for inline versus pad-site locations. The master template adapts to these variables without requiring a full re-engineering cycle, keeping your quoting and ordering process efficient.

sections.heading: National Account Advantages for Sign Shops
sections.content:
Winning a franchise sign program can transform a regional sign company's revenue profile. Instead of quoting one-off projects, you're billing recurring production orders with predictable specifications and established pricing. The key is having a wholesale manufacturing partner who also produces channel letters and cabinet signs who can deliver consistent quality at scale — without becoming your competitor.

Here's how partnering with Sunlite Signs strengthens your franchise program bids:

  • Pricing Stability: We provide volume-tiered wholesale pricing that you can build into long-term program agreements with confidence. No surprise cost increases mid-contract
  • Capacity: Our fabrication facility handles simultaneous multi-location orders without bottlenecking your other production work
  • Code Compliance: UL 48 listing on every illuminated unit means you clear electrical inspection at every location without variance requests or re-work
  • Speed: Three-week standard lead times and two-week batch rates mean you can commit to aggressive franchise buildout schedules

Many of our trade partners have built their franchise programs from a single-location prototype order into 50+ unit annual accounts. The foundation is always the same: a product that matches the brand standard, arrives on time, passes inspection, and doesn't generate callbacks. That's what Sunlite Signs delivers on every order.

faqs.question: Can you store franchise brand templates for repeat orders?
faqs.answer: Yes. We create and maintain master template files for every franchise brand we produce. Templates include cabinet engineering, LED layout, face artwork, and paint specifications. Reorders reference the template for identical output with zero re-engineering time.

faqs.question: What is the minimum order for franchise volume pricing?
faqs.answer: Volume pricing tiers begin at 5 units per order. Batch orders of 10 or more qualify for additional discounts and compressed production schedules. Contact us with your projected annual volume for a custom pricing proposal.

faqs.question: How do you handle size variations across franchise locations?
faqs.answer: Our master templates are designed with scalable parameters. We can adjust cabinet dimensions, LED module count, and mounting hardware for different site conditions while maintaining exact brand-standard color and proportional accuracy across all sizes.

faqs.question: Do you ship directly to job sites for multi-location rollouts?
faqs.answer: Yes. We can ship individual units directly to each job site with site-specific labeling, packing lists, and installation documentation. This eliminates the need for your shop to receive, sort, and re-ship units for geographically dispersed programs.

faqs.question: Will you contact our franchise clients directly?
faqs.answer: Never. Sunlite Signs is a wholesale-only manufacturer. We do not solicit, contact, or market to your end clients under any circumstances. Your customer relationship is yours — we are your silent manufacturing partner.

title: Custom Shaped Logo Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale custom-shaped logo signs with precision contour-cut aluminum cabinets. Any shape, any brand. German-engineered, UL 48 listed, 3-week lead time.
heroSubtitle: Precision contour-cut logo cabinets that follow every curve, angle, and detail of your client's brand mark. No shape is too complex.

sections.heading: Turning Any Logo into a Precision Sign Cabinet
sections.content:
The defining feature of a logo box is its contour — the cabinet silhouette that traces the exact outline of the brand mark. This is what separates a logo box from a rectangular cabinet sign with applied graphics. The contour demands precision engineering. When done right, the contour cut makes the sign look like the logo itself is mounted on the building, not a box with a logo on it. The visual difference is dramatic, and it's why custom-shaped logo signs are the format of choice for brands that take their identity seriously.

At Sunlite Signs, we specialize in translating complex vector artwork into manufacturable sign cabinets. Our process begins with your client's logo file — typically an AI or EPS — which our engineering team analyzes for fabrication feasibility. We assess minimum radii for aluminum bending, identify areas that require welded segment joints, and determine optimal cabinet depth for the given contour complexity. The output is a detailed shop drawing that shows exactly how the finished sign will look, including all dimensional callouts and mounting details.

Our CNC plasma and routing systems cut aluminum components to tolerances of ±0.02", ensuring the finished cabinet is a faithful reproduction of the digital artwork. Curved sections are formed on precision brake presses or roll-formed to maintain smooth, kink-free profiles. Every joint is TIG-welded, ground smooth, and finished so the seams disappear under paint. The result is a seamless cabinet that looks like it was stamped from a single piece of metal.

sections.heading: Engineering Complex Contours
sections.content:
Not all logo shapes are created equal from a fabrication standpoint. A rounded oval is straightforward; a detailed mascot with thin appendages, sharp interior angles, and isolated elements requires significantly more engineering. Sunlite Signs has the manufacturing expertise to handle both ends of the complexity spectrum — and everything in between.

  • Interior Cutouts: Logos with enclosed negative space (like the letter "O" or a circular emblem with a center icon) require internal returns and independent face panels. We engineer these as integrated components of the main cabinet, maintaining structural rigidity without visible seams
  • Thin Extensions: Elements like antenna, tails, or script flourishes that extend beyond the main logo mass are reinforced with internal aluminum gussets to prevent flexing or vibration in wind
  • Multi-Part Assemblies: Some logos are best fabricated as two or more separate cabinets that mount together on the wall. This approach simplifies shipping, reduces installation weight per piece, and allows for mixed illumination effects (e.g., face-lit icon with halo-lit wordmark)
  • Dimensional Depth Variation: We can vary cabinet depth across a single sign — deeper sections for areas needing more illumination uniformity, shallower sections for thin graphic elements

Our engineering team provides a fabrication feasibility review with every quote, including recommendations for structural reinforcement, cabinet segmentation, and mounting strategy. This upfront engineering eliminates surprises during production and ensures the finished sign meets both aesthetic and structural requirements.

sections.heading: From Concept to Installed Sign
sections.content:
The custom-shaped logo sign workflow at Sunlite Signs is designed to keep your project on track from first contact through installation. Here's how a typical project moves through our facility:

  • Step 1 — Artwork Review: You submit the client's logo file. Our team evaluates it for fabrication viability and returns a preliminary quote within 24 hours
  • Step 2 — Engineering Drawings: Upon order, we produce full shop drawings showing cabinet contour, depth, LED layout, mounting system, and all critical dimensions. You approve the drawings before production begins
  • Step 3 — Fabrication: CNC cutting, forming, welding, and paint in our climate-controlled facility. LED modules and power supplies are installed and tested
  • Step 4 — Quality Check: Every sign undergoes a 24-hour burn-in test and visual inspection against the approved shop drawings
  • Step 5 — Shipping: Signs are crated in custom-built wooden frames with foam cushioning for damage-free transit. Mounting hardware and documentation ship in a separate labeled box

Standard production is three weeks from drawing approval. Complex multi-part assemblies or signs exceeding 10 feet in any dimension may require an additional week. Rush production is available for time-critical projects — ask your account representative about current scheduling capacity.

faqs.question: What file formats do you need for custom-shaped logo signs?
faqs.answer: We prefer vector files in AI, EPS, or PDF format. These provide the clean outlines needed for CNC programming. If only raster files (JPG, PNG) are available, our art department can recreate the logo as a vector for an additional charge.

faqs.question: Is there a minimum or maximum size for custom-shaped logo boxes?
faqs.answer: We manufacture custom-shaped logo signs from approximately 18 inches up to 20 feet in the longest dimension. Signs exceeding 8 feet are typically fabricated in sections and field-assembled. There is no minimum order quantity — we build single units at wholesale pricing.

faqs.question: How do you handle logos with very thin or delicate elements?
faqs.answer: Thin extensions like script flourishes or antenna are reinforced internally with aluminum gussets to prevent wind-induced flexing. We may also recommend slight proportional adjustments to ensure the element is structurally sound without visibly altering the logo design.

faqs.question: Can custom-shaped logo signs be both illuminated and non-illuminated?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer face-lit, halo-lit, combination-lit, and non-illuminated configurations for any custom shape. Non-illuminated units use the same precision cabinet construction and can be paired with external gooseneck lighting if nighttime visibility is needed.

faqs.question: What is the lead time for a complex custom-shaped logo sign?
faqs.answer: Standard production is three weeks from approved drawings. Highly complex multi-part assemblies or oversized signs may require four weeks. Rush scheduling is available — contact us with your project timeline for a production date commitment.

title: Outdoor Logo Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale outdoor logo boxes built for extreme weather. Welded aluminum, sealed LED systems, UV-stable finishes. UL 48 listed, 3-week delivery for sign trade.
heroSubtitle: Weatherproof contour logo cabinets engineered to perform in rain, sun, wind, and temperature extremes. Built for years of exterior service without fading or failure.

sections.heading: Engineering Logo Boxes for Exterior Environments
sections.content:
Outdoor logo boxes face a punishing combination of environmental stressors that interior signs never encounter. UV radiation degrades pigments and plastics. Proper structural engineering is essential. Rain and humidity infiltrate poorly sealed cabinets, corroding electrical connections and promoting mold growth on light-diffusing surfaces. Wind loads stress mounting hardware and can flex thin cabinet walls enough to crack face panels. Temperature cycling causes expansion and contraction that loosens fasteners and breaks adhesive bonds over time.

At Sunlite Signs, every outdoor logo box is engineered specifically for exterior service — not adapted from an interior product with afterthought weatherproofing. Our approach starts with fully welded 0.090" aluminum cabinets with continuously welded seams that eliminate potential water ingress paths. We don't rely on silicone caulk or gasket tape to keep water out; the cabinet itself is a sealed enclosure by design.

The face retainer system uses a mechanically captured trim cap rather than adhesive-mounted retainers that can release under thermal cycling. Internal LED modules are rated to IP65 or higher, and wiring connections use marine-grade butt splices with adhesive-lined heat shrink. The result is a logo box that shrugs off years of weather exposure without the color fading, water intrusion, or electrical failures that plague lower-quality alternatives.

sections.heading: Materials, Finishes & UV Protection
sections.content:
Material selection is the first line of defense — backed by our complimentary engineering against outdoor degradation. Sunlite Signs specifies every component of an outdoor logo box for long-term exterior durability:

  • Cabinet Material: 5052-H32 marine-grade aluminum, selected for its superior corrosion resistance compared to standard 3003 alloys. All welded construction — no rivets or screws that create galvanic corrosion points
  • Face Material: UV-stabilized acrylic (Plexiglas or equivalent) with minimum 10-year outdoor warranty against yellowing and crazing. For high-impact environments, we offer polycarbonate faces with hard-coat UV protection
  • Paint System: Three-stage automotive-grade finish — etching primer, polyurethane base coat, and UV-blocking clear coat. We specify PPG or Sherwin-Williams exterior-grade urethanes with a minimum 7-year gloss retention warranty
  • Graphics: First-surface digital prints are laminated with UV-protective overlaminate. Second-surface prints are inherently protected by the acrylic face panel
  • Hardware: All mounting bolts, standoffs, and brackets are stainless steel or hot-dip galvanized to prevent rust staining on the building facade

We test our finishes against ASTM B117 salt spray standards and Florida 45-degree UV exposure benchmarks. The signs we build for a coastal installation in Miami perform just as well as the ones mounted in a northern climate with freeze-thaw cycling — because the materials and construction methods are specified for worst-case conditions regardless of the project location.

sections.heading: Wind Load Engineering & Structural Compliance
sections.content:
Outdoor signage is subject to wind load requirements established by local building codes, typically referencing ASCE 7 (Minimum Design Loads for Buildings) — the same standard applied to illuminated blade signs or the International Building Code (IBC). Logo boxes, with their large face areas and sometimes irregular contour shapes, can generate significant wind forces that must be transferred through the mounting system into the building structure.

Sunlite Signs provides wind load calculations with every outdoor logo box order. Our structural engineering covers:

  • Effective Wind Area: Calculated from the overall cabinet dimensions and contour shape, accounting for the actual face area exposed to wind pressure
  • Design Wind Speed: Based on the project location's ASCE 7 wind speed map zone, with exposure category adjustments for building height and surrounding terrain
  • Mounting System: Engineered bracket or cleat systems with connection capacities verified against the calculated wind forces. We specify anchor types based on the wall substrate — expansion anchors for concrete, through-bolts for steel, and lag bolts with structural backing for wood-frame construction
  • Safety Factor: All structural connections are designed with a minimum 2:1 safety factor above the calculated design load

For jurisdictions that require stamped engineering drawings as part of the sign permit application, we provide PE-stamped structural calculations and mounting details prepared by licensed engineers. This documentation is included as a standard part of our outdoor logo box package, not an upcharge — because we believe code compliance should be built into every project, not treated as an add-on.

faqs.question: What IP rating do your outdoor logo box LED systems carry?
faqs.answer: All LED modules in our outdoor logo boxes are rated IP65 or higher, meaning they are fully protected against dust ingress and low-pressure water jets. Combined with our sealed welded cabinets, this ensures reliable electrical performance in any weather condition.

faqs.question: How long do outdoor logo box finishes last before fading?
faqs.answer: Our three-stage automotive-grade paint system carries a minimum 7-year gloss retention warranty. Face materials are UV-stabilized acrylic with a 10-year warranty against yellowing. Real-world performance typically exceeds these ratings significantly.

faqs.question: Do you provide wind load calculations for outdoor signs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every outdoor logo box order includes wind load calculations based on the project's geographic location, building height, and exposure category per ASCE 7 standards. PE-stamped engineering drawings are available for jurisdictions that require them for permitting.

faqs.question: Can outdoor logo boxes be mounted on pylon or monument structures?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We engineer mounting systems for wall, pylon, monument, and freestanding pole applications. Each mounting configuration is structurally designed for the specific installation scenario and wind load requirements.

faqs.question: What warranty do you offer on outdoor logo boxes?
faqs.answer: We provide a 5-year warranty covering manufacturing defects in materials and workmanship, including the LED system and power supply. Paint finish and face materials carry their own manufacturer warranties as detailed in our specification sheets.

title: LED Logo Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale LED logo boxes with Samsung and Nichia modules. Energy-efficient, 50,000-hour lifespan, UL 48 listed. German-engineered with 3-week delivery.
heroSubtitle: High-efficiency LED illumination engineered for uniform brightness, long life, and low operating cost. The modern standard for illuminated logo signage.

sections.heading: LED Technology Inside Modern Logo Box Signs
sections.content:
LED modules have completely replaced fluorescent and neon as the illumination standard for logo box signs, and for good reason. LEDs consume 40-60% less electricity than fluorescent equivalents, run significantly cooler (reducing heat-related cabinet degradation), and deliver a rated lifespan of 50,000+ hours — which translates to roughly 11 years of continuous dusk-to-dawn operation. For sign shops, this means fewer warranty callbacks and happier clients.

But not all LED implementations are created equal. The difference between a well-engineered LED logo box and a poorly designed one is immediately visible: hot spots near the module locations, dark zones between rows, and color inconsistency across the face panel. These are symptoms of improper module selection, incorrect spacing, or insufficient cabinet depth for the LED's beam angle to fully disperse before reaching the face.

At Sunlite Signs, LED engineering is a core competency, not an afterthought. We calculate module placement using optical modeling software that accounts for cabinet depth, face translucency, and the specific beam angle of the selected LED module. The result is edge-to-edge uniform illumination with no visible hot spots or dead zones — the kind of quality that distinguishes professional-grade signage from commodity product. Every LED system we install is UL recognized, paired with UL-recognized Mean Well drivers, and integrated into a UL 48 listed assembly.

sections.heading: LED Module Selection & Performance Specs
sections.content:
Sunlite Signs stocks and specifies LED modules from Samsung and Nichia — the two manufacturers that consistently deliver the highest luminous efficacy, color consistency, and long-term lumen maintenance in the sign industry. Here's what we specify for our logo box illumination systems:

  • Luminous Efficacy: 160+ lumens per watt, significantly exceeding the 100-120 lm/W range of budget modules. Higher efficacy means less power consumption and less heat generation for the same brightness level
  • Color Temperature: Standard options at 6500K (daylight white), 5000K (neutral white), and 4000K (warm white). Custom color temperatures and RGB modules are available for specialty applications
  • Color Rendering Index (CRI): 80+ CRI standard, with 90+ CRI available for applications where accurate color reproduction through the sign face is critical
  • Lumen Maintenance: L70 rating of 50,000 hours minimum, meaning the modules will still produce at least 70% of their initial light output after 50,000 hours of operation
  • Operating Temperature: Rated for -40°F to +140°F, covering the full range of North American outdoor installations

We pair every LED module array with a Mean Well constant-voltage power supply, sized with a 20% power headroom margin to avoid running the driver at full load. This derating practice significantly extends driver life and reduces the already-low failure rate. Power supplies are mounted on accessible service trays or in remote junction boxes depending on the cabinet configuration.

sections.heading: Energy Savings & Total Cost of Ownership
sections.content:
For sign shops quoting LED logo boxes to cost-conscious clients, the total cost of ownership argument is compelling — see sign ROI for businesses. While LED logo boxes carry a moderate premium over non-illuminated alternatives, the operating cost savings and zero-maintenance LED lifespan deliver a strong return on investment over the sign's service life.

Consider a typical 4' x 6' logo box comparison:

  • Fluorescent System: Six T12HO lamps at 75W each = 450W total draw. Annual energy cost at $0.12/kWh running 12 hours per day = approximately $236/year. Lamp replacement every 2-3 years at $15-25 per lamp plus service call labor
  • LED System: Samsung modules at 160 lm/W producing equivalent brightness at 140W total draw. Annual energy cost = approximately $73/year. No lamp replacements for 11+ years of dusk-to-dawn operation
  • Annual Savings: $163 in electricity plus eliminated maintenance visits. Over a 10-year period, the LED system saves approximately $2,000+ in total operating costs

These numbers make LED logo boxes an easy sell for franchise operators, property managers, and any client paying their own electric bill. The energy reduction also supports LEED and green building certifications — an increasingly important consideration for commercial real estate projects.

Sunlite Signs can provide energy consumption data sheets for any logo box configuration, giving your sales team concrete numbers to include in client proposals. We also supply the power consumption and heat dissipation specifications that electrical engineers need for circuit planning on new construction projects.

faqs.question: What LED brands do you use in your logo boxes?
faqs.answer: We specify Samsung and Nichia LED modules exclusively. Both manufacturers deliver industry-leading luminous efficacy (160+ lm/W), exceptional color consistency, and L70 lumen maintenance ratings of 50,000+ hours. We do not use budget or no-name LED modules.

faqs.question: How much electricity does an LED logo box consume?
faqs.answer: Power consumption varies by size and brightness level, but a typical 4' x 6' LED logo box draws approximately 140 watts — about 60% less than a comparable fluorescent system. We provide exact wattage specifications with every quote for electrical planning purposes.

faqs.question: Can LED logo boxes be dimmed?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer 0-10V dimming-compatible power supplies that allow integration with building management systems, photocells, or manual dimmers. Dimming can further reduce energy consumption during low-traffic hours and extend LED module life.

faqs.question: What color temperatures are available for LED logo boxes?
faqs.answer: We stock modules in 6500K (daylight white), 5000K (neutral white), and 4000K (warm white). RGB modules are available for color-changing effects. Custom color temperatures can be sourced for projects with specific requirements.

faqs.question: How do you prevent hot spots in LED logo boxes?
faqs.answer: We use optical modeling software to calculate optimal module spacing based on cabinet depth, LED beam angle, and face material translucency. This engineering approach ensures uniform light distribution across the entire sign face without visible bright spots or dark zones.

title: Aluminum Logo Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale welded aluminum logo boxes with precision fabrication and seamless finishes. Lightweight, corrosion-resistant, UL 48 listed. 3-week delivery for trade.
heroSubtitle: Precision-welded aluminum logo cabinets that combine structural strength with lightweight elegance. The material of choice for professional sign fabrication.

sections.heading: Why Aluminum is the Premier Material for Logo Box Signs
sections.content:
Aluminum has been the dominant material in sign cabinet fabrication — from illuminated cabinet signs to logo boxes for decades, and logo boxes are no exception. The material offers a unique combination of properties that make it ideal for contour-cut sign cabinets: it's strong enough to maintain structural rigidity across large spans, light enough to simplify installation, and naturally resistant to the corrosion that destroys steel signs in exterior environments. When welded by skilled fabricators with the right equipment, aluminum also produces seamless joints that disappear under paint — giving finished logo boxes the appearance of a monolithic sculpture rather than an assembled box.

At Sunlite Signs, aluminum fabrication is the foundation of everything we build. Our shop runs dedicated aluminum TIG welding stations staffed by AWS-certified welders who work exclusively with sign-grade aluminum alloys. The difference between our welds and those of a general metal shop is visible in the finished product: our joints are smooth, consistent, and ground flush with the parent material before they ever see primer. No filler blobs, no grinding gouges, no warping from excessive heat input.

We stock 5052-H32 and 3003-H14 aluminum sheet in gauges from 0.063" to 0.125", pre-cut to standard sizes for immediate production start. Our CNC plasma and router tables handle the contour cutting, while our press brakes and roll-forming equipment shape the returns and curved elements that give each logo box its three-dimensional form.

sections.heading: Fabrication Process & Quality Standards
sections.content:
The fabrication sequence for a welded aluminum logo box follows a precise workflow — supported by complimentary engineering — designed to maximize structural integrity and surface quality:

  • CNC Cutting: Face panels, back panels, and return strips are cut from sheet stock on our CNC plasma table or router. Contour accuracy is held to ±0.02" tolerance. Internal structural gussets and LED mounting plates are cut in the same operation
  • Forming: Return strips are bent to match the contour profile using programmed press brake sequences. Curved sections are roll-formed on variable-radius equipment to eliminate the flat spots that cheapen the appearance of poorly fabricated signs
  • Welding: All joints are TIG-welded using ER5356 or ER4043 filler rod selected for the specific alloy combination. Weld penetration is verified against our internal spec — we require full fusion at every joint, not tack welds with gaps
  • Finishing: Welds are ground flush with 80-grit through 220-grit progressive sanding. The entire cabinet is chemical-etched, primed with self-etching epoxy primer, and finished with polyurethane paint in any specified color

We inspect every cabinet at three checkpoints: post-welding (structural), post-grinding (surface), and post-paint (final visual). Any unit that doesn't pass all three checks is re-worked before LED installation begins. This quality process adds time versus shops that weld, spray, and ship, but it's why our logo boxes arrive looking like a finished product rather than a work in progress.

sections.heading: Aluminum vs. Alternative Cabinet Materials
sections.content:
Sign shops occasionally consider alternative materials for logo box fabrication — steel, composite panels, or even plastics. Here's how aluminum compares — the same material used in our aluminum channel letters on the metrics that matter for logo box performance:

  • Aluminum vs. Steel: Aluminum weighs roughly one-third as much as steel at equivalent strength, reducing installation labor and mounting hardware requirements. Aluminum doesn't rust, eliminating the need for galvanizing or constant paint maintenance. Steel is only preferable when extreme vandal resistance is required
  • Aluminum vs. ACM (Aluminum Composite Material): ACM panels are popular for flat-face cabinet signs but cannot be welded into true contour shapes. ACM is folded and riveted, leaving visible seams at joints. Welded aluminum provides seamless contour reproduction that ACM cannot match
  • Aluminum vs. PVC/HDPE: Plastic cabinets are lighter and cheaper but lack structural rigidity at larger sizes, yellow under UV exposure, and cannot carry UL 48 certification for illuminated signs. They're not suitable for professional-grade logo boxes

For contour-cut logo boxes that need to reproduce complex brand marks with seamless surfaces, weather resistance, and long-term structural integrity, welded aluminum remains the clear professional choice. It's the material that lets you build a sign once and forget about it — which is exactly what your clients are paying for.

Sunlite Signs processes over 100,000 square feet of aluminum sheet annually across our logo box, cabinet sign, and channel letter product lines. That volume gives us purchasing leverage on raw materials that translates directly to competitive wholesale pricing for our trade partners.

faqs.question: What aluminum alloy do you use for logo box cabinets?
faqs.answer: We primarily use 5052-H32 for exterior applications due to its superior corrosion resistance and weldability. For interior or budget-sensitive projects, we use 3003-H14 which offers excellent formability at a lower material cost. Both alloys weld cleanly and accept paint finishes well.

faqs.question: What gauge aluminum do you use for logo box construction?
faqs.answer: Standard logo box cabinets use 0.080" aluminum for faces and backs, and 0.063" for returns. Large-format signs (over 6 feet) step up to 0.090" or 0.125" gauge for added structural rigidity. We select gauge based on cabinet size, mounting method, and wind load requirements.

faqs.question: Are welds visible on the finished logo box?
faqs.answer: No. Every weld is ground flush with the parent material through a progressive sanding sequence and then finished with primer and paint. The result is a seamless surface with no visible weld marks, grinding patterns, or joint lines.

faqs.question: Can aluminum logo boxes be finished in brushed or metallic effects?
faqs.answer: Yes. In addition to standard solid colors, we offer brushed metal, anodized-look, metallic, and textured paint effects. We can also apply genuine brushed aluminum laminate to specific face areas for an authentic metal aesthetic that shows through the logo design.

faqs.question: How do aluminum logo boxes handle coastal salt air environments?
faqs.answer: Excellently. Our 5052-H32 aluminum alloy is inherently corrosion-resistant, and our fully welded construction eliminates the rivets and fastener holes where galvanic corrosion typically initiates. Combined with our automotive-grade paint system, these cabinets deliver years of service in coastal environments.

title: Full Color Logo Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale full-color logo signs with precision digital printing. Photo-quality brand reproduction on illuminated contour cabinets. UL 48 listed, 3-week delivery.
heroSubtitle: Photo-quality digital printing that reproduces every gradient, shadow, and color transition in your client's brand mark. No compromises on color fidelity.

sections.heading: Digital Printing Meets Precision Sign Fabrication
sections.content:
Many brand logos are far more complex than a simple two-color wordmark. Modern brand identities frequently incorporate gradients, photographic elements, metallic effects, drop shadows, and multi-color palettes that are impossible to reproduce using traditional cut-vinyl or painted methods. Full-color logo signs solve this challenge by combining high-resolution digital printing with the structural precision of contour-cut aluminum cabinets, delivering a sign that reproduces every nuance of the brand file with photographic accuracy.

At Sunlite Signs, our digital printing workflow is calibrated specifically for sign face applications — which have different requirements than standard wide-format printing. Sign faces must maintain color accuracy when backlit by LED modules, which shifts perceived color temperature and saturation compared to reflective (front-lit) viewing. We use ICC profiles tuned for translucent substrates and LED backlight color temperatures, so the colors your client approves on a proof match what they see on the finished, illuminated sign.

Our print capabilities cover the full range of logo complexity — from simple four-color process (CMYK) to expanded gamut printing with white ink under-base for vibrant colors on non-white substrates. We print first-surface (front of the face panel) and second-surface (behind the face panel) depending on the durability and optical requirements of the specific application. Every printed face is finished with a UV-protective overlaminate that guards against fading, scratching, and environmental degradation.

sections.heading: Print Technology & Color Management
sections.content:
Color accuracy in full-color logo signs depends on three factors: the print technology, the color management system, and the substrate/backlight interaction. Our electrical engineering ensures consistent backlighting. Sunlite Signs controls all three to deliver consistent, brand-standard results on every unit — supporting franchise logo programs and one-off projects alike.

  • Printer: We use UV-curable wide-format printers that produce 1200 dpi resolution with exceptional dot placement accuracy. UV inks cure instantly under LED lamps, producing a durable, scratch-resistant ink film that doesn't require additional drying time
  • Color Management: Our RIP software uses custom ICC profiles built for each face material and backlight combination. We proof every job on calibrated monitors and can provide physical laminated color proofs for brand-critical projects
  • White Ink: For translucent faces that will be backlit, we print a controlled white ink layer behind the color image. The density and coverage of this white layer determines how the backlit and daylight appearance balance — too much white blocks the light, too little allows the LEDs to wash out the colors. We calibrate this for every project

The substrate selection also impacts color performance. We print on optically clear cast acrylic, opal translucent acrylic, and translucent polycarbonate depending on the illumination requirements. Second-surface printing (on the back of a clear face panel) produces the most vivid colors because the ink is protected behind the acrylic and viewed through a glossy surface that enhances saturation and depth. First-surface printing is used when the face panel is translucent and the print must be visible from the front without looking through the substrate.

sections.heading: Durability & Outdoor Performance of Printed Logo Faces
sections.content:
The most common question sign shops ask about digitally printed logo faces is: how long will the print last outdoors? It's a fair concern — our wholesale clients expect long-term performance — early generations of solvent and eco-solvent inks had significant fading issues, particularly with magenta and yellow channels that are UV-sensitive. Modern UV-curable inks have largely solved this problem, but print longevity still depends on the entire system, not just the ink.

Sunlite Signs specifies a complete durability stack for every full-color logo sign:

  • UV-Curable Inks: Our UV inks are rated for 5+ years of outdoor exposure without significant color shift, per accelerated weathering tests (SAE J2527). This rating assumes unprotected exposure — lamination extends it significantly
  • Protective Overlaminate: Every first-surface print receives a UV-filtering clear overlaminate that adds an additional 3-5 years of fade protection, physical scratch resistance, and chemical resistance against cleaning agents
  • Second-Surface Protection: Prints applied to the back of clear or translucent acrylic are inherently protected from physical damage, UV degradation, and chemical exposure by the face panel itself. Second-surface prints routinely last 8-10 years in outdoor service
  • Face Panel UV Stability: All acrylic face panels are UV-stabilized with a minimum 10-year warranty against yellowing and crazing from sun exposure

For franchise programs and national accounts where long-term color consistency is critical, we recommend second-surface printing on clear acrylic with a white ink under-base. This configuration delivers the best combination of color vibrancy, backlit performance, and outdoor durability — and it's the approach used by most major QSR and retail brands for their logo box signage programs.

faqs.question: What print resolution do you use for logo sign faces?
faqs.answer: We print at 1200 dpi using UV-curable wide-format printers. At typical viewing distances for building-mounted signs (10+ feet), this resolution produces photo-quality results with no visible dot pattern or banding.

faqs.question: How do you ensure printed colors match the brand standard when backlit?
faqs.answer: We use custom ICC profiles calibrated for each face material and LED backlight color temperature combination. Backlit viewing shifts color perception, so our profiles compensate for this effect to ensure the illuminated sign matches the approved brand colors.

faqs.question: What is the difference between first-surface and second-surface printing?
faqs.answer: First-surface printing places ink on the front of the face panel — suitable for translucent substrates. Second-surface printing places ink on the back of a clear panel, protecting the print behind the acrylic. Second-surface offers superior durability and richer color saturation.

faqs.question: How long do printed logo faces last outdoors?
faqs.answer: With our UV-curable inks and protective overlaminate, first-surface prints are rated for 7-10 years of outdoor service. Second-surface prints on clear acrylic typically last 8-10+ years. Actual lifespan varies by geographic exposure conditions.

faqs.question: Can you print gradients and photographic elements on logo box faces?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our 1200 dpi UV printing reproduces continuous-tone gradients, photographic imagery, metallic effects, and fine detail with photo-quality results. There are no limitations on color count or complexity — if it exists in the digital file, we can print it.

title: Double-Sided Logo Boxes | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale double-sided logo boxes for pole, pylon, and monument mounting. Dual-face illumination, welded aluminum, UL 48 listed. 3-week delivery for sign trade.
heroSubtitle: Dual-face logo cabinets that capture attention from both directions of traffic. Engineered for pole, pylon, and monument installations.

sections.heading: When Single-Face Visibility Isn't Enough
sections.content:
Not every sign installation has the luxury of facing a single direction of traffic. Pole-mounted signs along roadways, double-sided blade signs, monument signs at property entrances, and median-positioned pylons all serve traffic approaching from opposite directions. A single-face logo box in these applications wastes half its potential audience — drivers coming from the opposite direction see nothing but a blank aluminum back panel. Double-sided logo boxes eliminate this limitation by presenting the brand identity on both faces simultaneously.

Double-sided logo boxes are structurally different from their single-face counterparts. The cabinet requires two face panel openings, internal LED illumination that serves both sides without cross-contamination, and a structural internal framework that maintains rigidity across a wider cabinet depth. The mounting system must also account for significantly higher wind loads, since the double-face configuration acts as a larger sail area than a single-face unit of the same dimensions.

At Sunlite Signs, we engineer double-sided logo boxes as purpose-built assemblies — not two single-face cabinets bolted back to back. Our approach uses a single welded aluminum cabinet with internal divider that creates two independent illumination chambers. This construction method is lighter, stronger, and thinner than the bolted-together alternative, and it produces cleaner sight lines from the edge profile view. Every double-sided unit is UL 48 listed and ships ready for your crew to mount and wire.

sections.heading: Internal Engineering for Dual-Face Illumination
sections.content:
The engineering challenge unique to double-sided logo boxes is managing illumination for two faces within a single cabinet — our electrical engineering optimizes each chamber. If LED modules are mounted on a flat center divider, each face receives its own dedicated light chamber — this is the simplest approach and the one we use most frequently. However, some designs require more sophisticated solutions:

  • Center Divider System: A solid aluminum panel bisects the cabinet, creating two sealed chambers. LEDs mount on each side of the divider, facing their respective face panels. This is the standard configuration for cabinets 6" deep or greater, providing ample LED-to-face distance for uniform illumination
  • Shared Module Configuration: For thin-profile cabinets under 6" deep, we use dual-emission LED modules that project light in both directions from a single strip. This eliminates the center divider, reducing cabinet depth while still achieving acceptable face illumination
  • Independent Circuit Control: Each face can be wired to a separate driver circuit, allowing one side to be dimmed or turned off independently. This is useful for signs near residential areas where one face is visible from homes and may need nighttime dimming to comply with light pollution ordinances

We calculate LED layout for each face independently because the two sides may have different graphics — a common scenario when one face displays the brand logo and the other displays address information or a secondary brand mark. Different graphic densities and color distributions can require different LED spacing to achieve consistent brightness across both faces.

All power supplies are mounted in accessible compartments — typically at the bottom of the cabinet or in an external junction box on the mounting pole. Our wiring layout ensures that a technician can service one face's LED array without disturbing the other side's system.

sections.heading: Mounting Systems for Poles, Pylons & Monuments
sections.content:
Double-sided logo boxes are most commonly installed on pole mounts, pylon structures, and monument sign bases. Each mounting scenario has distinct structural and aesthetic requirements that Sunlite Signs addresses during the engineering phase:

  • Direct Pole Mount: The logo box attaches to a round or square steel pole using a welded internal bracket system. We engineer the bracket to accept standard pole diameters from 4" to 12" and distribute the sign's wind load evenly into the pole structure. The pole penetration is concealed inside the cabinet for a clean appearance
  • Pylon Cabinet Integration: Double-sided logo boxes frequently serve as the tenant ID panels within a multi-tenant pylon structure. We fabricate the logo box to fit the pylon's internal track or bolt pattern, with dimensional tolerances that ensure a flush fit with the pylon's uprights and crossbars
  • Monument Base Mount: For ground-level monument signs, the double-sided logo box sits atop a masonry, stone, or aluminum base structure. We provide a bottom-mount cleat system that secures the cabinet to the base and routes electrical conduit through the base interior into the sign cabinet

Wind load engineering is particularly critical for double-sided installations because these signs typically stand on elevated poles or exposed structures where wind forces are highest. Our structural calculations account for the full effective wind area of both faces, applying the appropriate ASCE 7 coefficients for double-face sign configurations. We provide PE-stamped engineering drawings when required for permitting, covering the sign cabinet, mounting hardware, and connection to the support structure.

faqs.question: Can each side of a double-sided logo box display a different graphic?
faqs.answer: Yes. Each face is independently fabricated, so you can have different logos, brand marks, or messaging on each side. We engineer the LED layout for each face separately to account for differences in graphic density and color that affect illumination requirements.

faqs.question: What is the typical cabinet depth for a double-sided logo box?
faqs.answer: Standard double-sided cabinets range from 6" to 12" deep, with a center divider creating two independent illumination chambers. For thin-profile applications, we offer cabinets as shallow as 4" using dual-emission LED modules that eliminate the center divider.

faqs.question: How do you handle wind loads on double-sided pole-mounted signs?
faqs.answer: We calculate wind loads using the full effective area of both faces per ASCE 7 standards for double-face sign configurations. The mounting bracket, pole connection, and foundation (if applicable) are all engineered with a minimum 2:1 safety factor above the design wind load.

faqs.question: Can the two sides of a double-sided logo box be controlled independently?
faqs.answer: Yes. We can wire each face on a separate driver circuit, allowing independent dimming or on/off control. This is commonly requested for installations near residential areas where one face may need reduced brightness at night.

faqs.question: Do double-sided logo boxes cost significantly more than single-face units?
faqs.answer: Double-sided units require additional face material, LED modules, and a center divider or dual-emission modules, so they do cost more than single-face equivalents. However, the cost is substantially less than purchasing two separate single-face signs. Contact us for project-specific pricing.

title: Large Format Logo Signs | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale large-format logo signs for buildings and high-rises. Multi-section fabrication, crane-ready engineering, UL 48 listed. 3-week delivery for sign trade.
heroSubtitle: Oversized contour logo cabinets engineered for buildings, parking structures, and high-rise installations. Multi-section fabrication for any scale.

sections.heading: Scaling Brand Identity to Building Proportions
sections.content:
When a brand mark needs to be visible from a quarter mile away, standard sign sizes are inadequate. Large-format logo signs — cabinets exceeding 8 feet in any dimension and often reaching 15 to 20+ feet — transform building facades into brand billboards that command attention from highways, across shopping districts, and throughout urban skylines. These are the signs that define a location's identity from a distance, guiding customers to their destination long before they can read any supporting text.

Large-format logo signs — rivaling illuminated cabinet signs in scale present unique engineering challenges that separate experienced manufacturers from shops attempting to scale up their standard processes. Structural loads increase exponentially with size. Shipping constraints impose maximum dimensions that require field-assembled multi-section designs. Illumination uniformity becomes harder to maintain across large face areas. And installation typically requires crane lifts, engineered rigging plans, and coordination with building structural engineers.

Sunlite Signs has manufactured large-format logo cabinets for major commercial developments, automotive dealerships, hospital campuses, and mixed-use properties across the country. Our German-engineered fabrication standards scale to any size requirement — we use the same precision welding, CNC-cut components, and quality control processes whether the sign is 3 feet or 30 feet. Every large-format unit ships with structural engineering documentation, installation procedures, and UL 48 listing where illuminated.

sections.heading: Multi-Section Engineering & Field Assembly
sections.content:
Signs exceeding approximately 8 feet in their longest dimension typically cannot ship as a single unit due to freight carrier size restrictions and practical handling limitations. Sunlite Signs engineers large-format logo boxes as modular multi-section assemblies that are fabricated as individual components in our facility and joined in the field during installation.

  • Section Planning: We divide the logo into logical sections based on natural break points in the design — between characters, at color transitions, or along geometric lines where a seam will be least visible. Each section is small enough for standard freight shipping (typically under 8' x 6' per section)
  • Alignment System: Mating sections use precision-machined steel alignment pins and interlocking flanges that ensure perfect registration when assembled on the wall. There is zero guesswork for your install crew — the pieces fit one way, and only one way
  • Seam Concealment: Section joints are designed with overlapping trim caps or concealed splice plates that hide the seam line. After field assembly, the joint is virtually invisible at the sign's intended viewing distance
  • Electrical Continuity: LED circuits in each section terminate in weather-rated quick-connect plugs that span the section joints. The electrical system is pre-tested in our facility with all sections connected before disassembly and shipping

We provide a detailed assembly sequence drawing with every multi-section order, showing the installation crew exactly which section mounts first, where the alignment pins engage, and how the electrical connections route between sections. Large-format installations are typically crane-assisted, so our documentation includes rigging point locations and recommended pick weights for each section to support your crane operator's lift planning.

sections.heading: Structural Requirements for Building-Mounted Installations
sections.content:
Mounting a large-format logo sign to a building facade introduces structural forces that must be transferred through the building's cladding system into the primary structure — steel columns, concrete shear walls, or reinforced masonry. This is not a situation where toggle bolts and good intentions will suffice. The sign's weight, wind load, and seismic forces (in applicable zones) must all be resolved by an engineered attachment system.

Sunlite Signs provides comprehensive structural engineering for every large-format installation:

  • Dead Load: Large aluminum logo cabinets can weigh 200-800+ lbs depending on dimensions and configuration. We calculate total weight per section and per attachment point to ensure the building structure can support the static load
  • Wind Load: Large face areas generate substantial wind pressures and suctions. We apply ASCE 7 wind speed maps with height and exposure adjustments to calculate design forces, then engineer the mounting system with a minimum 2:1 safety factor
  • Seismic Load: For installations in seismic design categories C through F, we include seismic force calculations per ASCE 7 Chapter 13 (non-structural component provisions) and design the mounting connections accordingly
  • Building Interface: We coordinate with the building's structural engineer or architect to identify suitable attachment points — typically steel embed plates in concrete walls, direct connections to structural steel, or engineered transfer brackets that span between studs in metal-framed walls

All structural documentation is prepared under the supervision of a licensed Professional Engineer and can be wet-stamped for permitting in any U.S. jurisdiction. For sign shops bidding large-format projects, this engineering package is a powerful differentiator — it demonstrates to property owners and general contractors that your sign program is professionally engineered, not just visually designed.

faqs.question: What is the maximum size logo box you can manufacture?
faqs.answer: There is no practical maximum size. Individual cabinet sections are fabricated up to approximately 8' x 6' for shipping, then field-assembled into any overall dimension. We have produced logo signs exceeding 30 feet in length using modular multi-section construction.

faqs.question: How do you maintain illumination uniformity on very large signs?
faqs.answer: We map LED module placement independently for each section using optical modeling, ensuring uniform brightness across the full assembly. LED spacing, cabinet depth, and face material translucency are calibrated together to eliminate hot spots and dark zones, even at 20+ foot spans.

faqs.question: Do large-format logo signs require crane installation?
faqs.answer: Most large-format signs require crane or boom lift assistance for safe installation. We provide rigging point locations and section weights in our installation documentation so your crane operator can plan lifts efficiently. Some modular sections may be light enough for scaffold-based installation.

faqs.question: Can you provide PE-stamped structural engineering for large-format signs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every large-format logo sign includes structural calculations covering dead load, wind load, and seismic forces where applicable. PE-stamped drawings are available for permitting in any U.S. jurisdiction and are included as a standard part of the project package.

faqs.question: How are multi-section signs shipped?
faqs.answer: Each section is individually crated in custom-built wooden frames with foam cushioning. Sections are numbered and labeled to match the assembly drawing. Hardware, connectors, and documentation ship in a separate labeled crate. We arrange freight logistics for multi-crate shipments.
